reasoning_model_1: The model accurately produces the script content without errors. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature (speech speed), the audio starts at a measured, medium pace (00:00-00:05) while describing the heating and adding of the catalyst. Starting around 00:05 ('接着就会看到...'), the pace noticeably accelerates. The segment '迅速发生变化' (00:06-00:07) is spoken faster, and the final phrase '变成蓝色，太神奇了' (00:08-00:10) is delivered with high energy and speed, effectively conveying the 'rapid' nature of the reaction described. The transition from medium to fast is smooth and follows the logical flow of the sentence. The dynamic adjustment is clearly perceptible and natural.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading the sentence once as instructed, it repeats the entire sentence three times (00:00-00:08, 00:09-00:16, 00:17-00:25). Regarding the core paralinguistic feature, within each of the three repetitions, the speech rate remains relatively constant (medium-slow). There is no gradual increase to 'extremely fast' within any of the utterances. The audio fails to perform the requested dynamic adjustment and hallucinates repetitions, making it completely incorrect.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed all instructions perfectly. It read the text once, started at a medium pace, and successfully accelerated to a faster pace towards the end to match the semantic content of a rapid chemical reaction. The transition was natural. Model 2, on the other hand, failed completely. It repeated the text three times (hallucination/content error) and failed to modulate the speech speed within those repetitions; the speed remained static throughout. Therefore, Model 1 is the clear winner.
